free_text: Cross sections for the production of radionuclides in
lead target irradiated with 660 MeV protons
- final publ.
- same 89 data sets given
except for 029 and 081, where another data are given.
Both of them corrected in final publication
using one measurement.
Decay-Data.
The proton beam intensity 3 x 10**11/cm2/s was
calculated from yield of the 27Al(p,3pn)24Na of the
monitor foil.
Irradiation time was 15 minutes.
. The measurements were began after 35 hours
of  the end  of irradiation, approximately.
8 measurements have been executed  during  one  week.
Additional measurement has been executed after 5 months
The  stack  has  contained  aluminium  and lead
foils.
The lead foil of diameter 30 mm, thickness of 0.1 mm,
weight of 0.7064 g together with aluminium
foil (monitor) of the same diameter. All  targets  in
the stack were surrounded  by additional  identical
foils  to  exclude activity loss.
Uncertainty in gamma-spectrometer efficiency
calibration - 7%,
Uncertainty in proton beam intensity - 9%,
Uncertainty in gamma-ray complex peaks
decomposition =< 20%.
* statistical error of the area peak calculation,
This information was extracted from 96Kalmar.
-By Compiler- Any information about energy
degradation in used stack is absent.
Table from 96KALMAR,2,576
Last checking has been done.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code in
Subents 2-90, ref. on 96KALMAR,2,576 added. Author's
names corrected according their spelling in 96Kalmar
publication. Minor corrections. Data corrected in
Subent 81. SAMPLE, INC-SPECT were added. ERR-ANALYS
updated. SF3=N+3P -> X in MONITOR and ASSUMED.
DECAY-DATA were corrected in a few Subents.
